Font is selected in the `.kps` Keyboards tab if a font is included in Files
content.criteria.10_10-kps-description-m: Description is meaningful (becomes the keyboard home page content)
content.criteria.10_11-kps-no-spelling-e: No spelling errors in user-facing `.kps` text fields (Description, Author name, WebSite text)
content.criteria.10_2-kps-language-tags-: Language tag(s) are selected in the `.kps` Keyboards tab
content.criteria.10_3-kps-tags-minimal.d: Language tags are minimal — no unnecessary script/region subtags
content.criteria.10_4-kps-unusual-tag-fl: If the tag is new or unusual, it is flagged for possible submission to langtags/Ethnologue
content.criteria.10_5-kps-conlang-flagge: If keyboard is for a constructed language, it is flagged for special review
content.criteria.10_6-kps-numeric-region: If the language tag includes numeric regional codes, it is flagged for special review
content.criteria.10_7-kps-details-files-: `readme.htm`, `welcome.htm`, and `LICENSE.md` are selected in the `.kps` Details tab License field
content.criteria.10_8-kps-follow-keyboar: "Follow keyboard version" is selected in the `.kps` Details tab
content.criteria.10_9-kps-description-no: Description field is present and non-empty in the `.kps`
content.criteria.11_1-php-exists-with-he: `<name>.php` file exists and has the proper PHP header
content.criteria.11_10-php-htm-style-par: CSS in `welcome.htm` and `.php` are identical modulo non-rendering whitespace
content.criteria.11_2-php-osk-all-layers: If using OSK syntax, all desktop and mobile layers are listed in `data-states` (complementary to 11.6 — see that entry for the direction split)
content.criteria.11_3-php-style-inside-h: All style information is inside the PHP header, not outside it
content.criteria.11_4-php-no-closing-bod: `.php` does not contain closing `</body>` or `</html>` tags
content.criteria.11_5-php-htm-html-wellf: HTML in `welcome.htm` and `.php` is well-formed — balanced tags, proper headings, no unclosed elements (in-browser check via DOMParser)
content.criteria.11_6-php-osk-data-state: `.php` `data-states` attribute lists every layer defined in the `.kmn` and touch layout (complementary to 11.2: 11.2 catches layers missing from `data-states` — under-documentation; 11.6 catches `data-states` values naming layers that don't exist — phantom layers)
content.criteria.11_8-php-osk-image-cons: OSK graphics either use Keyman-generated form or custom images have width constraints
content.criteria.11_9-php-htm-body-parit: `welcome.htm` and `.php` body content are identical after stripping headers and normalizing whitespace
BCP47 tag is correct for the language and script
content.criteria.12_2-no-iso-639-5-codes: No ISO 639-5 collective language codes used
content.criteria.12_3-no-numeric-region-: No numeric regional codes unless explicitly contrastive
content.criteria.12_4-no-001-region.desc: Region code `001` is never used in BCP47 tags
content.criteria.12_5-script-mismatch-fl: If a language uses a different script than what langtags currently records, the mismatch is flagged
content.criteria.12_6-script-mismatch-su: If a script mismatch was flagged, I have opened an issue at github.com/silnrsi/langtags documenting the mismatch.
A flagged script mismatch has been submitted to langtags
content.criteria.13_1-pua-keyboard-in-ex: PUA codepoint use means keyboard is in `experimental/` (encoding/script context; see also 7.12 which checks the same condition during source review)
content.criteria.13_2-pua-notices-in-doc: PUA codepoint use means explicit PUA notices appear in readme.htm, welcome.htm, .php, and .kps description
content.criteria.13_3-no-unencoded-scrip: Keyboard does not reuse existing codepoints for unencoded scripts; if it does, warnings are in place
content.criteria.13_4-no-math-latin-look: No mathematical Latin look-alike characters (U+1D400-U+1D7FF) in keyboard output
content.criteria.13_5-math-latin-not-spo: If math-Latin or other Latin look-alikes are present, the use case does not look like spoofing
content.criteria.14_1-third-party-patch-: If this is a patch to another author's keyboard, the original author was consulted and has approved the change before this PR was opened
If a third party submits a patch to an existing keyboard, the original author was consulted and approved
content.criteria.15_1-relicense-permissi: The original author has confirmed permission to relicense this keyboard under MIT — written confirmation is referenced in this PR or on file
Original author has granted permission to relicense under MIT
content.criteria.15_2-new-keyboard-creat: New keyboard has been created in `release/` (renamed if needed)
content.criteria.15_3-legacy-listed-as-d: Legacy package ID is listed in the new keyboard's `.kps` Related packages as Deprecated
content.criteria.15_4-deprecated-md-crea: A `DEPRECATED.md` file exists in the legacy keyboard's folder naming the replacement
content.criteria.15_5-php-title-includes: The deprecated keyboard's help `.php` title includes "(deprecated)"
content.criteria.16_1-partner-notes-foll: Partner package's "note for keyboard maintainers" has been followed
content.criteria.16_2-partner-keyboards-: Keyboard version has been updated in the partner's `keyboards.csv` under `oem/`
content.criteria.17_1-build-trigger-pr-c: I have resolved all PR comments before triggering a TeamCity build.
Author has resolved all PR comments before triggering a TeamCity build
content.criteria.17_2-new-author-local-p: For a first-time contributor: this PR has been pulled locally and the build verified clean before triggering TeamCity
For a new author's first PR, the PR was pulled locally and checked thoroughly
content.criteria.17_3-build-issues-fed-b: Any issues the TeamCity build caught that local review missed have been fed back to the author.
After the build runs, any issues it catches that local review missed were fed back to the author
content.criteria.18_1-longpress-within-l: No touch long-press menu offers more than 8 options (warning), and none exceeds the hard cap of 10
content.criteria.18_10-frequent-chars-re: Frequently used characters sit on easy-to-reach positions consistent with the script's structure
content.criteria.18_11-tested-with-real-: This layout has been tested with target users - good on paper does not equal good in practice
The layout has been tested with real target users before submission
content.criteria.18_12-meets-legal-acces: This keyboard complies with any legislated or accessibility requirements for its target locale — if you are unsure whether requirements apply, check before submitting.
The keyboard meets any applicable legislated, societal, and accessibility requirements for its locale
content.criteria.18_2-touch-rows-within-: Touch layout uses 4-5 rows on phone and 5 rows on tablet
content.criteria.18_3-keys-per-row-withi: Touch layout uses at most 10 keys per row on phone and 13 on tablet
content.criteria.18_4-control-keys-stabl: Backspace, Enter, and other control keys do not move or resize across touch layers
content.criteria.18_5-layer-switch-toggl: Every layer-switch key has a corresponding key that toggles back to the previous layer
content.criteria.18_6-inventory-fully-co: Every character in the confirmed linguist inventory is produced by some reachable input sequence in the draft keyboard
content.criteria.18_7-mandated-currency-: The locale's mandated currency symbol (from CLDR) is reachable on the keyboard
content.criteria.18_8-character-set-is-m: The character set was kept focused - extra characters were resisted and each addition was deliberate
content.criteria.18_9-rare-chars-discove: Rare characters are findable without experimentation, not buried on deep long-press menus alone
content.criteria.19_1-import-attribution: When a keyboard was adapted from a release/ keyboard, the PR body must include the attribution block (adapted-from path, commit SHA, round-trip status, opaque feature inventory). Auto-enforced by the output service at PR submission time.
content.criteria.19_2-import-attribution: When a keyboard was adapted from a release/ keyboard, HISTORY.md must contain an 'Adapted from <sourcePath>' bullet under the 1.0 entry. Auto-enforced by the scaffolder at emit time (D14, mandatory carrier).
content.criteria.1_1-no-files-outside-ke: No files modified outside the keyboard's own folder
content.criteria.1_10-kpj-current-format: `.kpj` file uses the current/newest project-file format (the scaffolder template bakes the current `.kpj` schema version, literal `version="10.0"` as of the Keyman Developer 17/18 era; bump this note when the format moves)
content.criteria.1_13-no-stray-scratch-f: No stray scratch or test files in the keyboard folder
content.criteria.1_3-keyboard-under-orga: Keyboard sits under an alphabetic or org-code organizing subfolder, not at the root of `release/`/`experimental/`
content.criteria.1_4-no-source-files-at-: No source files placed at the repository root
content.criteria.1_5-keyboard-id-not-alr: The keyboard ID (folder name) is not already used in `experimental/`, `legacy/`, or `release/`
content.criteria.1_6-id-does-not-contain: Keyboard ID, folder name, and display name do not contain the word "keyboard"
content.criteria.1_7-no-keyboard-info-fi: No `keyboard.info` file in the keyboard folder
content.criteria.1_8-no-duplicate-docs-s: No `docs/` or similar subfolder containing duplicate HISTORY/README/INSTALL files
content.criteria.1_9-no-duplicate-source: No duplicate copies of the same source file inside the keyboard folder
content.criteria.2_1-version-bumped-if-p: If any file that ships in the package was modified, the keyboard version was bumped
content.criteria.2_2-version-not-require: If only non-package files were modified, a version bump is not required
content.criteria.2_3-version-numeric-dot: Version number is dot-separated and entirely numeric (SemVer or CalVer)
content.criteria.2_4-no-leading-zero-in-: No version component has a leading zero
content.criteria.2_5-no-v-prefix-or-labe: Version has no `v` prefix and no non-numeric labels
content.criteria.2_6-new-version-strictl: New version is strictly greater than the previously deployed version
content.criteria.2_7-kpj-version-not-bum: The `.kpj` file's `<Version>` field has not been bumped (it's the file-format version, not keyboard version)
content.criteria.3_3-history-recent-chan: Most recent change entry is at the top of the file
HISTORY.md is cumulative — prior entries are preserved, not overwritten
content.criteria.3_5-history-entry-forma: Each entry follows `<version> (<YYYY-MM-DD>)` format underlined with hyphens and bullet items
content.criteria.3_6-history-version-mat: Top HISTORY.md entry version matches the version in the `.kmn` file
content.criteria.3_7-history-bullets-no-: HISTORY.md bullets do not reference files that no longer exist in the keyboard
content.criteria.4_1-copyright-present-w: Copyright statement is present with year(s)
content.criteria.4_2-copyright-year-rang: If updated across multiple years, copyright uses a year range
content.criteria.4_3-copyright-holder-is: The copyright holder named in LICENSE.md is the author or a legal entity the author is authorized to represent
Copyright holder is the author or a legal entity they are authorized to claim
content.criteria.4_4-org-legal-name-curr: If an organization is named, its current legal name is used
content.criteria.4_6-copyright-year-not-: Copyright year is not embedded in the `.kmn` or `.kps` copyright strings
content.criteria.4_7-copyright-holder-co: Copyright holder name is identical across `LICENSE.md`, `.kmn`, `.kps`, `README.md`, and `HISTORY.md`
content.criteria.4_8-year-range-starts-a: If a year range is used, it starts at the original year and extends to the current year
content.criteria.5_2-no-version-in-readm: No version number embedded in README.md, readme.htm, welcome.htm, or help .php
content.criteria.5_3-no-copyright-in-rea: No copyright year/statement in README.md, readme.htm, or welcome.htm
content.criteria.5_7-readme-targets-matc: Targets listed in README.md match those in the `.kmn`
content.criteria.6_2-readme-htm-short.de: `readme.htm` content is short — just a brief description
content.criteria.6_4-welcome-htm-describ: `welcome.htm` describes how to use the keyboard
content.criteria.6_5-welcome-htm-no-inst: `welcome.htm` does not include instructions for installing Keyman — references help URLs instead
content.criteria.6_6-welcome-htm-lang-at: `<html lang="...">` attribute in `welcome.htm` matches the keyboard's primary BCP47 tag
content.criteria.6_7-welcome-htm-not-pol: `welcome.htm` content is not political or self-promoting
content.criteria.7_10-kmn-stores-balance: All `.kmn` string-literal stores have balanced quotes and valid syntax
content.criteria.7_11-author-in-kps-not-: Author name belongs in the `.kps`, not in a `.kmn` Author store
content.criteria.7_12-pua-in-experimenta: If keyboard uses PUA codepoints, it is in `experimental/` (source review pass; see also 13.1 which checks the same condition from the encoding/script angle)

content.criteria.7_14-pua-font-displayma: If keyboard ships a PUA-encoded font, `.kmn` includes `store(&DISPLAYMAP)` path
content.criteria.7_15-osk-reviewed.descr: OSK has been reviewed for rules, output characters, and layout consistency
content.criteria.7_16-kvks-not-blank.des: The `.kvks` on-screen keyboard layout is populated, not blank
If a special font is needed for output characters, an appropriate font is selected in the OSK
content.criteria.7_18-touch-layout-revie: Touch layout reviewed for layers, output, and fingering
content.criteria.7_19-identical-phone-ta: If both phone and tablet touch layouts are identical, removal of one was requested
content.criteria.7_2-targets-any-alone.d: `.kmn` targets line uses `any` alone, not combined with individual platform names
content.criteria.7_20-same-font-phone-ta: Same font is selected for phone and tablet layouts
content.criteria.7_21-compiles-no-errors: Keyboard compiles with no errors (build-output context; errors only, not warnings — see 7.6 for the stricter source-review check)
content.criteria.7_3-targets-no-unsuppor: `.kmn` targets do not include `mobile` unless a touch layout exists
content.criteria.7_4-modifier-names-cons: Modifier key names consistent across `.kmn`, `.kvks`, and `.keyman-touch-layout`
content.criteria.7_5-ralt-usealtgr-tag.d: If keyboard uses RALT, the `.kvks` includes the `usealtgr` tag
content.criteria.7_6-compiles-no-errors-: Keyboard compiles with no errors and no warnings (source review pass; stricter than 7.21 which checks errors only)
content.criteria.7_7-display-name-no-und: Keyboard display name is a readable phrase that describes the keyboard (judgment half; the mechanical underscore check is 7.7a)

content.criteria.7_8-rtl-flag-matches-sc: RTL flag in `.kmn`/`.kps` matches the script's actual writing direction

content.criteria.8_13-kps-targets-match-: Targets declared in `.kmn` match compiled files listed in the `.kps`
content.criteria.8_2-kps-includes-kvk.de: Compiled `.kvk` is listed in the `.kps` package
content.criteria.8_3-kps-includes-js-if-: Compiled `.js` is listed if a mobile layout exists
content.criteria.8_6-kps-includes-welcom: `welcome.htm` and associated graphics are listed in the `.kps` package
content.criteria.8_7-kps-includes-requir: All required fonts referenced by the `.kmn` and touch layout are listed in the `.kps` (family-level presence check; full typeface — Bold/Italic — completeness is best-effort, not mechanically guaranteed)
content.criteria.8_8-kps-no-php-file.des: No `.php` help file listed in the `.kps` package
content.criteria.8_9-kps-no-help-folder-: No files from the `help/` folder listed in the `.kps` package
content.criteria.9_1-font-license-nonemp: Every bundled font has a non-empty license field in the `.kps`
content.criteria.9_2-font-license-open-i: Font license identifier matches a known Open license (OFL, GPL, MIT, Apache, CC-BY, etc.)
content.criteria.9_3-font-license-genuin: Font license text/spirit is genuinely Open, not just labeled "freeware"
content.criteria.9_4-shared-font-in-shar: If a font is used by more than one keyboard, it lives under `shared/fonts`
content.criteria.9_5-shared-font-current: I have verified the shared font path points to the current shared/fonts location in keymanapp/keyboards, not a deprecated duplicate.
